result.stdout:
Mklinker 2.1.3
Copyright (c) 2020

  addlink        Adds a new link to config file

  addlinks       Adds multiple new links at once to config file with optional
                 filtering

  addvar         Adds a new variable to config file

  removelink     Removes link from config

  removevar      Removes a variable from config file

  linkall        Generates all links from config

  list           Lists all the links or variables in the config

  config         Displays information about config file by default, but can
                 create / delete / modify config file

  validate       This will validate the config file to see if it is valid

  interactive    Starts an interactive session where you can run multiple
                 commands without 'Mklinker' in front. Use 'exit' to exit from
                 interactive session

  scan           Detect if circular paths (loops) exist for a given root folder
                 by scanning all directories and collecting data. This detection
                 is very rough and will only give an indication by displaying
                 how often certain directory names are used (indicating loops
                 around those directory names)

  help           Display more information on a specific command.

  version        Display version information.

commands.payload:
Mklinker 2.1.3
Copyright (c) 2020

  addlink        Adds a new link to config file

  addlinks       Adds multiple new links at once to config file with optional
                 filtering

  addvar         Adds a new variable to config file

  removelink     Removes link from config

  removevar      Removes a variable from config file

  linkall        Generates all links from config

  list           Lists all the links or variables in the config

  config         Displays information about config file by default, but can
                 create / delete / modify config file

  validate       This will validate the config file to see if it is valid

  interactive    Starts an interactive session where you can run multiple
                 commands without 'Mklinker' in front. Use 'exit' to exit from
                 interactive session

  scan           Detect if circular paths (loops) exist for a given root folder
                 by scanning all directories and collecting data. This detection
                 is very rough and will only give an indication by displaying
                 how often certain directory names are used (indicating loops
                 around those directory names)

  help           Display more information on a specific command.

  version        Display version information.

result.stdout:
Mklinker 2.1.3
Copyright (c) 2020

  -f, --force     (Default: false) If this flag is set it will ignore validation
                  checks and add it no matter what

  -p, --path      (Default: linker.config) Specifies path to config file

  --help          Display this help screen.

  --version       Display version information.

  value pos. 0    Required. The path to the source file

  value pos. 1    Required. The path to new link file

  value pos. 2    (Default: Default) The type of link you want to create.
                  Default is Symbolic for files and Junction for directories

commands.payload:
Mklinker 2.1.3
Copyright (c) 2020

  -f, --force     (Default: false) If this flag is set it will ignore validation
                  checks and add it no matter what

  -p, --path      (Default: linker.config) Specifies path to config file

result.stdout:
Mklinker 2.1.3
Copyright (c) 2020

  -r, --regex             (Default: [\s\S]*) Regex filter deciding which files /
                          directories to add links for in source folder. It
                          matches based on file / directory name only. Default
                          will match everything

  -a, --absolute-regex    (Default: [\s\S]*) Additional Regex filter deciding
                          which files / directories to add links for in source
                          folder. It matches based on absolute (full) path.
                          Default will match everything. Both regex filters have
                          to be matched in order for file / directory to be
                          added

  -s, --subdirs           (Default: false) Determines if files (not directories)
                          from subdirectories are included as well (recursive)

  -d, --dirs              (Default: false) Determines if directory links should
                          be created instead of file links.

  -p, --path              (Default: linker.config) Specifies path to config file

  --help                  Display this help screen.

  --version               Display version information.

  value pos. 0            Required. The path to the source directory

  value pos. 1            Required. The path to the target directory

  value pos. 2            (Default: Default) The type of link you want to
                          create. Default is Symbolic for files and Junction for
                          directories

result.stdout:
Mklinker 2.1.3
Copyright (c) 2020

  -l, --limit      (Default: 30) Maximum amount of subfolders used for detecting
                   loop (recursion limit). Higher values might result in more
                   time, but will be more accurate

  -v, --verbose    (Default: false) Will display detailed output including every
                   path that has reached the recursion limit

  -i, --ignore     (Default: false) Will ignore folders Mklinker does not have
                   access to and continue the scan

  --help           Display this help screen.

  --version        Display version information.

  value pos. 0     (Default: .) The root folder that should be scanned. Default
                   will scan current working directory
